Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 4, 135 [Ša-zi-ga][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (1 column missing)
Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

DU₃.DU₃-BI KU.KU AN.BAR IGI-lim PIŠ₁₀.dID₂ UḪ₂.dID₂

(o ii 1) Its ritual: you pound together iron powder, imhur-līm-plant, kibrītu-sulphur (and) ruʾtītu-sulphur; you pour (them) into oil; you recite this incantation 7 times for (your) desire.

o ii 22

1niš SUD₂ ana lib₃-bi I₃ ŠUB EN₂ an-nit 7šu₂ ana ŠA₃ ŠID

o ii 33

ša₂ NITA GIŠ₃-šu₂ ša₂ MUNUS GAL₄.LA-šu₂ ŠEŠ₂-su

(o ii 3) The man's penis, the woman's pudenda, you annoint them (lit. it).

o ii 44

EN₂ ŠA₃.ZI.GA MIN<(ŠA₃.ZI.GA)> KI.NU₂ ŠA₃.ZI.GA

(o ii 4) Incantation: "Sexual desire, sexual desire, the bed of desire:

o ii 55

i-⸢pu-uš⸣ diš-tar ana dDUMU.ZI

(o ii 5) Ištar made (it) for Dumuzi;

o ii 66

[i-pu]-⸢uš⸣ dna-na-a ana ḫa-i-ri-šu₂

(o ii 6) Nanaya made (it) for her (lit. his) consort;

o ii 77

[i-pu]-⸢uš⸣ diš-ḫa-ra ana dal-ma-ni-šu₂

(o ii 7) Išhara made (it) for her (lit. his) Almanu;

o ii 88

[e-pu]-⸢uš⸣ ana-ku a-na NENNI A NENNI

(o ii 8) I, myself, made (it) for so-and-so, descendant of so-and-so.

o ii 99

[li-iḫ]-⸢mu⸣-u₂ UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ li-zaq-qi-pu u₂-šar-šu₂

(o ii 9) May his flesh get stiff! May his penis become erect!

o ii 1010

[...] mu-ši u ur-ri ina qi₂-bit iq-bu-u AN.ZIB₂

(o ii 10) [...] night and day, at the command that the skilful Ištar decrees, Nanaya, Gazbaba and Kanisura". The wording of the incantation.

o ii 1111

[diš]-tar dna-na-a dGAZ.BA.BA dKA.NI.SUR.⸢RA⸣ TU₆ EN₂

o ii 1212

[DU₃].DU₃-BI UZU.DIR KUR.RA IGI-lim ṣa-ṣu-un-tu₂ 3 U₂-[ḪI.A ...]

(o ii 12) Its ritual: you pound a mountain kamūnu-fungus, an imhur-līm-plant, a ṣaṣumtu-plant, 3 plants [...]; you pour (them) into an pūri-bowl (for) oil; a censer of burāšu-juniper [...]

o ii 1313

[...] SUD₂ ana ŠA₃ I₃ BUR ŠUBdi NIG₂.NA šim⸢LI⸣ [...]

o ii 1414

[...] x EN₂ an-nit 3šu₂

(o ii 14) [...] ... this incantation three times.

(rest of column missing)
Reverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
r i 1'r 1'

[...] x

(r i 1') [...] ...

r i 2'2'

[...] la₃ TEe

(r i 2') [...] do not approach!

r i 3'3'

[...] ina kur-kur-ra-tu ina KUŠ BAR-ši₂

(r i 3') [...] you released it into a kurkurratu-vessel (or) into a leather (bag).

r i 4'4'

[...] IGI-lim kib-rit ina KUŠ

(r i 4') [...] imhur-līm-plant, kibrītu-sulphur, into a leather (bag)

r i 5'5'

[...] x u₂tar-muš u₂IGI-lim u₂IGI.20

(r i 5') [...] lupin, imhur-līm-plant, imhur-ešrā-plant

r i 6'6'

[...] ⸢NU?⸣ tar-muš KA tam-tu₂ AN.BAR I₃ MAN.<DU>

(r i 6') [...] ... lupin, algae, iron ...

r i 7'7'

[...] ina GU GADA E₃ ina GU₂-šu₂ GAR

(r i 7') you thread [...] on a linen string; you put (it) around his neck.

r i 8'8'

[...] ⸢NITA₂?⸣ na₄URUDU NITA₂ AN.BAR NITA₂ ina KUŠ

(r i 8') [...] male [...], male copper, male iron, into a leather (bag)

r i 9'9'

[...] x-MEŠ * NA ina MAŠ₂.GE₆-šu₂ UŠ₂-MEŠ IGI.IGI

(r i 9') [...] .... (If) a man keeps seeing dead men in his dream.

r i 10'10'

[...] ⸢DU₁₁⸣.DU₁₁ub SIG₂ BABBAR SIG₂ SA₅ NU.NU E₃

(r i 10') You speak [...]; you plait white wool and red wool; you thread (them);

r i 11'11'

[...] ⸢TAR?⸣ U₄.DA DU₈ U₄.DA BAD

(r i 11') [...] ... "May the fever be cleared away! May the fever leave!

r i 12'12'

[...] egir nam-tar-ra al-gub

(r i 12') [...] the sickness does stand back!"

(2 columns missing)
